topic: An artist discovers someone has tattooed their work
raw_output:
[story 1]

Elara traced the edge of the canvas, her knuckles white against the rough linen. The scent of turpentine and linseed oil usually brought a calm focus, but today, a tight knot was coiling low in her stomach. She had just finished the portrait, a swirling depiction of twilight on water, and as she stepped back, her breath hitched. On the lower right corner, subtly integrated into the reflection of the water, was a miniature, intricate tattoo—a design she didn't recognize, yet felt undeniably *hers*. A tremor ran through her hand, causing the brush to tremble. She blinked rapidly, trying to rationalize the sight, but the silence of her studio amplified the frantic drumming in her chest. Who had done this? And why?

[story 2]

Marcus adjusted his spectacles, the movement jerky and uncharacteristic. He stared at the printout of the latest commission, a vibrant cityscape rendered in bold strokes. Then his gaze snagged on a detail near the base of the building—a delicate, almost hidden script woven into the shadow work. A cold slickness spread across his skin. He swallowed hard, the sound loud in the quiet room. He leaned closer, his posture rigid, his eyes darting from the ink to the gallery wall. A strange, unsettling awareness washed over him; it felt like being observed, cataloged. He wanted to retreat, to hide the evidence, but his feet seemed rooted to the floor, unwilling to move.

[story 3]

Clara smoothed the fabric of her apron repeatedly, a repetitive motion that seemed to anchor her to the spot. The new piece, an abstract study in deep indigo, was drying under the harsh studio lights. As she wiped a streak of pigment from her forearm, she noticed it—a precise, fine line of black ink curving around her wrist, mimicking the flow of the paint. Her heart began to beat a frantic rhythm against her ribs. She froze, her breath catching in her throat. Every instinct screamed at her to look away, to pretend she hadn't seen the mark. She slowly raised her gaze, her jaw tight, the air thick and heavy around her. She couldn't seem to form a coherent thought, only a desperate urge to vanish.
